- Advanced Analytics, Alteryx, UiPath
- ,
- 12 Dec 2025

# Three Tools CFOs Can Use to Jumpstart Their AI Journey

Explore how CFOs can jumpstart their AI journey with Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath. Learn how these tools streamline data management, automate processes, and drive smarter financial decision-making for measurable business impact.

Advanced AnalyticsAgentic AIAICFOGenerative AIPredictive AI

As a CFO, your role extends well beyond managing finances – you’re at the forefront of driving automation, improving efficiency, and shaping **[strategic transformation](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinar/drive-your-automation-journey-with-alteryx-and-capitalizes-office-of-finance-blueprint/)**. With countless AI tools and platforms available, it’s challenging to identify where to begin. From streamlining data collection to leveraging machine learning, the opportunities are vast, but finding the technologies that deliver real value requires a focused approach.

Let’s demystify one of the most common buzzwords: Artificial Intelligence (AI). At its core, AI means having computers perform tasks traditionally done by humans. The real appeal? _Saving time_. Take ChatGPT, for example – it helps generate text quickly and efficiently, freeing up valuable hours. Similarly, implementing AI solutions allows you to tackle tasks faster and more effectively, maximizing productivity.

While Hollywood portrays AI as futuristic or even apocalyptic, the reality today is far more practical. Today, AI is about using smart tools to solve real-world problems and drive value. From foundational data collection to advanced automation and decision-making, the AI journey offers transformative potential at every step.

To stay ahead, you need to cut through the noise and focus on technologies that deliver meaningful impact. At Capitalize, we’ve seen how time-saving automation and smarter data management can revolutionize businesses. Let’s dive into three powerful technologies – Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath – that can help your finance team streamline operations, make better decisions, and lay the groundwork for AI-driven success.

### Databricks: Building a Solid Data Foundation

The key to unlocking the full potential of AI lies in a strong data strategy – _a clear plan for collecting, organizing, and using your organization’s data effectively_. Two of the most common approaches to data storage are data lakes and data warehouses. Think of a data lake as a vast reservoir that stores raw, unfiltered data, while a data warehouse acts more like a refined library that organizes and processes data for easy access. Databricks offers a Lakehouse platform that combines the flexibility of data lakes with the structured efficiency of data warehouses, enabling seamless data integration and accessibility.

With **[Databricks](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/how-databricks-and-alteryx-work-together-to-accelerate-advanced-analytics/)**, you can:

- Consolidate disparate data sources (ERP systems, financial research data, external market data) into a centralized platform.
- Interact with the AI/BI Genie to engage with your data using natural language (aka plain English), enabling you to create dashboards, uncover insights, and accelerate data-driven initiatives.
- Take advantage of AutoML to develop baseline machine learning models (such as sales forecasting) with a glassbox approach where the code is generated automatically and ready to be refined to best fit your desired use case.
- Use the context-aware Databricks Assistant for help with generating, describing, or debugging code.
- Manage the entire lifecycle of **[Generative AI models](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/unlocking-the-power-of-gen-ai-with-databricks/)**, from data preparation and model training to deployment and monitoring, ensuring a seamless and scalable approach to AI implementation.

By centralizing data, leveraging AI-driven insights, and simplifying the adoption of machine learning, you can move beyond traditional analytics to drive strategic initiatives.

### Alteryx: Automating Data Prep and Analysis

Your finance team often spends hours – or even days – wrangling data in Excel, leaving little time for strategic analysis. Enter Alteryx, a no-code/low-code platform that democratizes analytics and automates tedious data preparation.

With **[Alteryx](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/alteryx/)**, you can:

- Automate data preparation and blending across multiple sources, reducing manual effort and errors while speeding up critical processes (like month-end reporting and profitability reviews).
- Streamline repetitive workflows with intuitive drag-and-drop tools, empowering your team to focus on higher-value tasks.
- Utilize AI-driven data transformation suggestions to identify the best ways to structure, clean, and optimize your datasets, making workflows more efficient and reducing the manual effort required for data preparation.
- Integrate easy-to-use predictive analytics and machine learning to identify trends, forecast outcomes, and guide strategic initiatives without requiring advanced coding skills.
- Leverage Alteryx Auto Insights to uncover actionable, AI-driven insights and visualizations that enable faster, data-backed decisions.

By enhancing data accessibility, making advanced analytics more approachable, and saving time through automation, you can uncover new opportunities for growth and efficiency.

### UiPath: Eliminate Repetitive Tasks with RPA

You may be thinking, “Isn’t RPA just automation? What does that have to do with AI?” While it’s true that Robotic Process Automation (RPA) focuses on automating repetitive tasks, modern platforms like UiPath have integrated powerful AI capabilities that go far beyond traditional automation.

With **[UiPath](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/what-is-uipath/)**, you can:

- Leverage AI-driven Intelligent Document Processing to extract, validate, and categorize financial data from unstructured documents like invoices, receipts, and contracts, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy.
- Utilize AI models for process optimization, enabling bots to intelligently adapt to complex workflows and make data-driven decisions.
- Combine RPA with natural language processing (NLP) to handle text-heavy tasks, such as reading and interpreting compliance reports or contracts.
- Seamlessly integrate with your existing ERP systems, CRMs, and other tools to streamline workflows and eliminate manual data entry.

Imagine a bot powered by AI not only logging into your ERP but also analyzing patterns in financial data, identifying anomalies, and preparing actionable reports for your team. UiPath transforms finance operations by blending automation with AI to deliver smarter, faster, and more accurate results.

### Simplify the Complexity

Navigating the AI landscape can feel overwhelming, but focusing on the right tools makes all the difference. Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath each offer unique capabilities that empower finance teams to unlock the full potential of their data and streamline operations. Whether it’s Databricks’ robust data infrastructure and AutoML tools, Alteryx’s user-friendly analytic tools and low-code predictive capabilities, or UiPath’s intelligent automation and document processing, these platforms enable CFOs to move beyond traditional methods and embrace data-driven decision-making.

By integrating these solutions into their tech stack, CFOs can not only optimize day-to-day processes but also position their organizations to thrive in an increasingly data-centric world. At Capitalize Consulting, we specialize in helping finance leaders implement these tools to drive measurable results.

Ready to get started? Contact Capitalize Consulting today to **[schedule a personalized demo](https://share.hsforms.com/1P4RLctPmRWmpbbTqWeQ61Qdko8c)** and see how Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath can revolutionize your finance operations.

# Optimizing Supply Chain Analytics Using Alteryx & UiPath

Explore how organizations optimize supply chain analytics to improve performance, resilience, and decision-making.

Agentic AI

Supply chain teams are dependent on external vendors/suppliers and massive internal software platforms to do their jobs. Working with so many teams and systems outside of the supply chain’s control makes manual processes and data challenges inevitable.

Dumping data from third parties and internal systems quickly overloads Microsoft Excel, and IT teams struggle to keep up with the constantly changing requirements and ad hoc requests of the supply chain team. Tasks that should take minutes take hours and what should take hours requires days or weeks. Some processes in supply chain are so complex, they are impossible with tools like Excel due to processing times and record limits.

Robotic process automation (RPA) tools like UiPath and self-service data and analytics tools like Alteryx give supply chain teams the power to automate processes and deal with massive data sets quickly and efficiently.

**_Why It Matters_**

With increased competition, more regulations, and tight job markets, process automation and fast data analysis are more important than ever before. The number of systems and the amount of data collected is expanding faster every day, and over the last several years, supply chains have been hit with unprecedented challenges – global conflict and pandemics, severe weather events and a changing climate, and previously unseen levels of port congestion. In the midst of this unpredictability, it’s critical to use the data you have at your disposal to make the best possible business decisions.

The benefits of doing so are clear. Optimized supply chains generally have 15% lower costs, less than 50% inventory holdings, and a faster cash-to-cash cycle by nearly three times compared to under-optimized supply chains.

**_Challenges to Overcome_**

Some of the key challenges we see while working with supply chain teams include:

1. Difficulty accessing and combining data from multiple sources including internal systems, vendor portals, and cloud systems.
2. Inconsistent data formats and dirty data that need to be cleansed and combined.
3. Difficulty getting the data ready for analysis because so much time is spent compiling and double-checking the data.
4. Requests for Tableau or Power BI dashboards, but the data challenges make it impossible to keep them running and current.
5. So much time spent with manual tasks and data entry, there is no time to do advanced data analysis and proper forecasting.

To alleviate the issues above, we need technology that can fetch data from anywhere, deal with the data, and push the data to reports, dashboards, internal, and external systems. Any time spent repeating manual, time-consuming, and error-prone tasks is a waste of time.

At Capitalize, we’ve helped hundreds of companies build repeatable, automated processes using two key technologies.

1. UiPath: Commonly called a “robotic process automation” or “RPA” platform, UiPath gives users the ability to train a “bot” that can mimic nearly any process that requires a person to use a keyboard and mouse. Opening applications, navigating websites, running reports, reading PDFs, filling out form, downloading files, and sending emails are just a few examples.
2. Alteryx: Can be classified as a data wrangling and data analytics tool. Alteryx can pull, cleanse, and combine data from nearly anywhere. For users who find themselves spending a lot of time fighting with Excel to merge, analyze, or output data, Alteryx is a game-changer.

**_How Process Automation with UiPath and Data Automation with Alteryx Can Help_**

By leveraging UiPath on manual processes and Alteryx on internal and external data, your supply chain teams can fully automate processes and handle large and complex data sets. You will build auditable, documented processes and stop spending valuable time performing tedious and repetitive tasks in multiple portals and Excel. Instead, your time can be spent on higher-value work: focusing on exceptions, gathering deeper analytical insights throughout the procurement process, reducing costs, and improving supplier diversity.

UiPath and Alteryx have the potential to transform every touchpoint in your supply chain, including Spend and Contract Analytics, Supplier Management, and Pricing and Compliance Analytics. It’s end-to-end. Integrating the tools together quite simply revolutionizes the process of exploring, cleaning, and combining data, for greater forecasting and visualization capability than has ever been possible. Used together, these tools help supply chain teams make fast and informed data-driven decisions, while repetitive tasks are automated in the background.

**_What is Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and UiPath?_**

RPA tools like UiPath allow you to create “bots” that replicate manual human actions. Using RPA tools, you can automate keyboard strokes and mouse clicks, log in and navigate websites or applications, fill out forms, monitor events, and call other processes as needed. This allows you to eliminate hours of repetitive tasks working with websites, and emails, pulling data from a variety of sources, managing and manipulating files, and processing documents.

RPA tools give you the ability to scale up without hiring more people, adding capacity to your existing team. Hours of manual processing time can be completely eliminated. They can dramatically increase speed and response times, reducing hours of delivery time to minutes or even seconds. And on top of all that, the automation processes dramatically reduce the risk of human errors that can result from tedious and repetitive work.

For example, one of your clients sends you a daily spreadsheet of orders, which must then be entered through a web portal or application. If there is no API, the team will need to manually type in each product and the quantity, and submit the orders. A UiPath bot has the capability to capture and document the orders, log into the product website or purchasing portal, and complete the order – all without human intervention. If there’s a problem with the order, the bot will notify the team to step in and assist.

In other cases, pulling data together for reporting can be difficult. For reports that require data from many systems, teams can end up running and downloading reports only to then be stuck manually combining everything. RPA tools like UiPath can be trained to go out to dozens of websites, plug in the correct dates, projects, and locations, export the reports to Excel, and then put them on a shared drive for the analysts to begin their work.

**_Self-Service Analytics: Alteryx_**

Once you’ve used an RPA tool to gather data, what do you do with it? Many teams pull everything into Excel and beat it into submission. It can be a daunting and onerous task.

Luckily, companies like Alteryx have built platforms that allow teams to quickly pull data from dozens of spreadsheets, systems, and APIs. You can wrangle data from various systems, clients, and vendors allowing you to cleanse and blend the data quickly. You can then transform it to create reports and dashboards. If needed, Alteryx gives you the ability to perform advanced geospatial and predictive analytics or to create highly sophisticated forecasting models.

For example: Your vendors provide weekly sales and inventory data as a report in Excel or CSV, and your historical sales records are stored in your ERP. You want to bring those datasets together and create a forecast of the products you need to ship to keep inventories above safety stock. Alteryx allows you to combine and analyze the data, and generate predictive models and forecasts, giving you a greater opportunity to ship exactly what the vendors need, exactly when they need it.

**_Putting It Together_**

When would you use UiPath and Alteryx together? Let’s say you have several datasets, all in different places – some in vendor portals, some in cloud systems, and some in your ERP. You want to use this data to determine what to order, and when.

For the vendor portals with no access directly to the database or an API, we would build a bot in UiPath that would log into each vendor portal, navigate to the correct report, run the report, export it to Excel, and move it to the shared drive.

With the files in Excel accessible on our network, we can use Alteryx to cleanse, modify, and merge the data with the data in our cloud system and our local ERP. The combined dataset can then be analyzed and output to dashboards, databases, or files depending on the business need.

If the output from Alteryx needs to be “hand keyed” into a different system, UiPath can pick up the file and type everything in, submit each page, and send updates when it’s complete.

By utilizing UiPath and Alteryx correctly, businesses can fully automate most data, analysis, and reporting processes, only intervening for the occasional exception!

**_The Power of Repeatable Processes_**

UiPath and Alteryx allow us to automate time-consuming, manual, error-prone, and monotonous processes. Each play a different role and not all processes need both technologies. In today’s high-paced, data-intensive world, understanding the tools available to automate processes is not an option, it’s a necessity.

By creating automated processes, you free up highly valuable team members so they can focus on creative ideas for cutting costs, increasing revenue, and keeping clients happy. Spending hours or days running reports, fighting with data in Excel, and hand-keying data into systems should not be how people work anymore!

With UiPath and Alteryx, we analyze a process, automate it, and put it on autopilot!

**_What’s Next?_**

If you’d like to know more, we’d love to connect. Our team is happy to create a use-case demonstration, tailored to your specific business requirements. If you’re as excited by the possibilities as we think you will be, we’ll set you up with trial licenses, allowing us to show proof of concept before moving into production. It all starts with a conversation. Feel free to touch base today at sales@capitalizeconsulting.com

# Integrate Alteryx and Snowflake

Learn how Alteryx Connect integrates with Snowflake to unify metadata, improve data discovery, and strengthen governance across analytics workflows.

Integration

Integrate Alteryx and Snowflake.mp4 from Capitalize on Vimeo

![video thumbnail](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/1401168703-b61cd2676bc5965537ae3457e746ca0ddb27ba00a0f924118d47aa14582116f3-d?mw=80&q=85)

Playing in picture-in-picture

Play

00:00

04:25

Settings

QualityAuto

SpeedNormal

Picture-in-PictureFullscreen

## **Integrate Alteryx and Snowflake**

Every company has large amounts of data that they need to store and analyze, but there can be many challenges in working with this data.Often the data is coming from different sources, is disorganized, and cannot be used immediately for analysis. Additionally, storing data requires significant system and resource management.

The good news – Alteryx and Snowflake can alleviate some of these pains by streamlining data preparation and optimizing database performance.

Let’s discuss then show you how!

## **Snowflake**

Snowflake is a cloud-based data warehouse that allows for efficient scaling and maintenance of databases. As queries are executed within Snowflake, the warehouse size can be scaled to account for different processing needs. There are multiple sizes that linearly improve the speed at which a query is executed.

The ability to adjust the warehouse size on an ad hoc basis allows users to process data quickly and systematically. Once databases are configured, Snowflake handles most of the database management such as query caching and system updates.

In short, Snowflake offers the standard data warehousing functionality in addition to optimizing storage and performance.

## **Connecting Alteryx to Snowflake**

With Alteryx,users can easily read and write data to Snowflake databases. The simplest  way to connect to Snowflake data is using the input and output tools. The only prerequisite is to set up and ODBC connection to a specific Snowflake web server. When the ODBC is configured and Snowflake credentials are provided users can read, write, create, and delete data tables. Users can also use the “In-Database” tools to connect to a database and directly manipulate the data, without having to pull and push data from Snowflake.

The Snowflake/Alteryx integration gives users more visibility and access to their data. The Alteryx platform eliminates the need for understanding SQL, so any user can process and analyze their data. Data can be pulled from many different sources within Alteryx, then it can be blended and transformed before updating a database, which provides more consistency for repetitive data uploads. Alteryx can also be used to analyze dataor send data to other sources, such as BI tools, for additional analysis, dashboarding, or data visualization.

Would you like to see/discuss how Alteryx and Snowflake work together and learn about best practices? Send us an email at [marketing@capitalizeconsulting.com](mailto:marketing@capitalizeconsulting.com), and let’s get it on the calendar!

# Scaling Self-Service Analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud

Scale self-service analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud to improve data governance, trust, and insight delivery across your organization.

Scaling Self-Service Analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud.mp4 from Capitalize on Vimeo

![video thumbnail](https://i.vimeocdn.com/video/1399897827-250deac85af09de2edf5b425d7caea13c3e436a6be1cc0af29b22b9f8f86e6e8-d?mw=80&q=85)

Playing in picture-in-picture

Play

00:00

47:40

Settings

QualityAuto

SpeedNormal

Picture-in-PictureFullscreen

[Webinar Slide Deck](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/Collibra-combined.pdf)

Self-service analytical solutions have changed the way companies access, analyze, and share data. People are able to pull data from nearly any source, manipulate it, and share it with anyone in minutes or hours instead of days or weeks.

However, with all great progress comes new challenges. Companies that provide self-service access can struggle with answering questions such as:

- Where are people getting their data?
- Are these data sources trusted?
- Who gave these folks access to these systems?
- Do the users understand what the data is telling them, what’s already been filtered, and what calculations have already occurred?

Collibra has designed a platform to help people find, access, understand, and control their data, easing the pain of large self-service environments. The solution includes:

- Data Catalog – Find, access, and trust the organization’s data with a simple interface that surfaces data assets across the organization.
- Data Governance – Organize, document, and certify data while collaborating with other users of that same data – in a controlled way.
- Data Lineage – See the source and any modifications that happen to your data as it makes its way to reports, dashboards, etc.
- Data Privacy – Ensure your data is staying private and you’re adhering to ever-changing privacy laws.

Please join Capitalize Analytics and Collibra in this webinar where we will discuss these challenges and how the Collibra platform makes self-service more efficient and safer.

data.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/using-wam-to-improve-workflows/#content)

- Alteryx Governance
- ,
- 30 Oct 2020

# Using WAM to Improve Workflows

Learn how Using WAM can improve workflows by streamlining work management, enhancing collaboration, and improving operational efficiency.

When building workflows in Alteryx, many users are not generally concerned with runtime and efficiency until after the initial workflow is built out and working properly. For example, best practices would suggest including/using Browse tools through the workflow to ensure that everything is working properly and that there are no outliers that are not accounted for. These Browse tools are very useful when troubleshooting the workflow, but when the workflow is moved to production, they require unnecessary processing and can greatly increase the workflow’s runtime. Alteryx Server accounts for this by disabling Browse tools when running workflows, but Designer does not, so manually removing Browse tools can be important for any workflow that is generally run via Designer. However, not all users are aware of this, while others may forget to remove or disable them, causing increased runtimes across the board.

One of the key features in WAM’s workflow Audit Reports is automated suggestions to improve workflows. These recommendations are included at the beginning of each Audit Report and aim to increase the performance and flexibility of workflows. The suggestions to improve performance include things like reducing field sizes and disabling Browse tools to reduce runtimes by decreasing the amount of data being processed.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1a.png)

A lesser known way to improve runtimes is to use Formula tools instead of Data Cleanse tools. WAM will always recommend replacing a Data Cleanse tool in the workflow with a Formula tool. This is because the Data Cleanse tool is a macro that includes ten Multi-Field Formulas. For larger datasets, this can greatly increase runtimes, so it is recommended to use a single Formula or Multi-Field Formula tool to cleanse data so that Alteryx does not have to process the entire dataset more than necessary. For example, one could use a simple replace formula to remove all the whitespace in a field. In the workflow below, a large dataset of over one million records is being cleansed by both the Data Cleanse and Formula tools, but the Data Cleanse tool takes an entire minute to run, compared to the three seconds that the Formula tool takes. When cleansing multiple fields this way, a Multi-Field Formula tool can be used with a runtime closer to the Formula tool rather than the Data Cleansing tool.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1a2.png)

Another way that WAM can help improve workflows is by suggesting changes that help with the workflow’s flexibility. To do this, WAM will always make a suggestion when a workflow includes an absolute path or an Auto Field tool. If absolute paths are used, the workflow will be unlikely to work for a different user without remapping the input and output files. This is because the file path will be different for different users, even for shared locations such as SharePoint. Instead, relative paths should be used to ensure that the input/output files are accessed relative to the workflow’s location. A useful article on relative file paths can be found here: [https://community.alteryx.com/t5/Alteryx-Designer-Knowledge-Base/How-To-Use-Relative-Paths/ta-p/1160](https://community.alteryx.com/t5/Alteryx-Designer-Knowledge-Base/How-To-Use-Relative-Paths/ta-p/1160)

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1a3.png)

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1a4.png)

Another way to improve a workflow’s flexibility is to use a Select tool instead of an Auto Field tool. Since the Auto Field tool automatically assigns the smallest data type and size for each tool, the data types and sizes may change when the workflow is run with different data. This can lead to issues further down the data stream. For example, a store has product codes that consist of letters and/or numbers. The store matches each day’s transactions to their database of product codes by using a Join tool. In the database, the product codes are saved as strings since they contain both letters and numbers, but one day, the store only has transactions consisting of product codes with only numbers. The Auto Field tool will change the data type of these codes to an Int16 instead of a string, which will then cause issues with the Join tool that is downstream, since String fields can only be joined to other string fields. Replacing the Auto Field tool with a Select tool that forces the product codes to be a string will solve this issue.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1a5.png)

Using the recommendations made by WAM can help users become accustomed to building more efficient, flexible workflows by pointing out areas of potential improvement. This helps users to decrease processing times as well as help them share workflows throughout their organization.

All Posts

[Skip to content](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/how-can-wam-help-me-with-change-control-and-versioning-on-my-alteryx-workflows/#content)

- Alteryx, Alteryx Governance
- ,
- 12 Oct 2020

# How can WAM help me with Change Control and Versioning on my Alteryx Workflows?

Learn how WAM improves Alteryx workflow change control and versioning by enhancing tracking, governance, and collaboration across teams.

Keeping track of the changes made to your workflows can be difficult, especially as they are being developed and tested iteratively. Saving multiple copies of workflows can also be confusing and clutter workspaces, and it can be difficult to distinguish the differences between each version. For example, an analyst has been working on a very complicated workflow, saving different versions after every large change to maintain some sense of version control, as well as the ability to reverse any unnecessary or incorrect changes. After a while, the folder containing the workflow has ten versions of the same workflow, with no way to determine the differences between them without going through each of them individually. Documenting the changes made every update to a workflow time consuming and subject to human error, so it would be helpful if this process could be automated to ensure accuracy and save time.

WAM allows users to track changes made to specific workflows through “commits” similarly to Git or any other version control system. To use this feature, simply navigate to the “Track Changes” section of  WAM and click “Add” to add a workflow. The workflows tracked using this feature of WAM do not have to be in the Workflow Path specified in the Settings like the workflows used in the Reporting or Mass Update sections.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Picture2new.png)

Once a workflow is being tracked, select the workflow under Tracked Workflows and click “Commit” to manually save the current version of the workflow. WAM will also parse the current version of each workflow being tracked every five minutes, so manual commits are not necessary. You will be able to view each saved version of the workflow by selecting the workflow as well. To stop tracking a workflow, click the “Stop Tracking” button. This will remove all saved versions aside from the “Main” version, so it is important to ensure that previous versions will not be needed or are already downloaded before performing this action.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Picture3new.png)

After selecting a tracked workflow, users can select past versions of workflows and download them individually. Versions downloaded this way are downloaded to the “Version Copies” folder that is made in the same folder as the original workflow. Additionally, users can choose to restore a past version as the “main” version – reverting the workflow back to the selected version. To view the changes made between versions, a Change History Report can be run by clicking “Download History.” These reports have three sections: Change History, Summary, and Details.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/3w3.png)

The Change History section lists each version of the workflow as well as when it was committed. This allows users to quickly get a timeframe as to when each version was saved, which can help narrow down which version that they are looking for.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/4w4.png)

In the Summary section, users can see a brief overview of the changes made between each saved version and the one before it. Any tool additions, deletions, or updates are listed here, but no specific tool configurations are shown in this section. This allows users to easily view when certain tools are added and is especially useful when branches are added to or removed from workflows.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/5w5.png)

The Details section of the report shows the configurations of the new or updated tools in a format similar to an Audit Report. This allows users to look for specific changes made to workflows, such as the changing of a formula or data type without having to compare audit reports side by side. Instead, this report shows each line that has changed between the current and previous version’s audit reports, even if they have not been run. With the workflow tracking feature of WAM, documenting and keeping track of changes to workflows is easy since version differences are automatically documented whenever a Change History report is run. The time normally spent on documenting changes to workflows and maintaining specific versions can then be spent on more meaningful tasks, such as improving the workflow and analyzing the results.

[Skip to content](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/how-to-quickly-verify-alteryx-workflow-versions/#content)

- Alteryx, Alteryx Governance
- ,
- 07 Oct 2020

# How to Quickly Verify Alteryx Workflow Versions?

Learn how to quickly verify Alteryx workflow versions to improve accuracy, enforce version control, and streamline analytics governance.

Alteryx Governance

Since Alteryx is always improving, it is important to keep track of which version of Alteryx Designer your workflows were created in. Not doing so can lead to version compatibility errors when trying to open workflows on an older version of Alteryx than the workflow was created in, since tool configurations sometimes get updated. It is also very important to keep track of which version of Designer your workflows are created in when saving them to your organization’s Gallery, as the Gallery is unable to run workflows that were created in a newer version of Alteryx.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/1q.png)

Additionally, new tools may be added with new versions of Alteryx. For example, the Intelligence Suite of tools were added with Alteryx version 2020.2. Opening a workflow with these tools on an older version will replace the incompatible tools as shown below.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/2q.png)

Alteryx’s backwards compatibility allows users to open old workflows in newer versions of Designer, but it can be hard to tell when a tool was updated for additional configurations. For example, a user has several workflows made in v2019 of Designer that use the Data Cleansing tool. Running the workflows in v2020 of Designer will yield the same results, but the Data Cleansing tool now has the added capability to remove null data that the user wants to incorporate into his or her workflow. Updating each workflow will take time, and it can be difficult to tell which workflow has been updated to include this new functionality. It would be useful to know which workflows were made in v2019 so that the user can know which ones still need to be updated.

WAM makes it easy to keep track of what version of Alteryx each workflow was made in by allowing users to create an Alteryx Versions Overview report. To do so, navigate to the Reporting tab of WAM, select the workflows that will be logged, and run the Alteryx Versions Overview report in the Management section.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/neww.png)

The report will then be saved in the “Management Reports” folder of the output folder specified in the settings. The report shows each version of Alteryx present in the selection as well as the file paths of the workflows made in each.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/4q.png)

Additionally, each audit report contains the version of the Alteryx workflow, so for workflows that are continuously updated, it is easy to tell which versions of the workflow correspond to which version of Designer.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/5q.png)

Using the version tracking feature of Audit Reports and Version Overview reports can give users insight into when workflows were last updated as well as which versions of Designer they are compatible with, since it can be difficult to keep everybody in an organization on the same version at all times. Compatibility issues can come up when sharing workflows with coworkers or uploading workflows to a server, so it is important to know which workflows might cause these issues beforehand. This also helps users keep track of the workflows that could be updated with new functionality or improvements that come with newer versions of Alteryx.

[Skip to content](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/easy-back-up-of-workflows-in-wam/#content)

- Alteryx, Alteryx Governance
- ,
- 28 Sep 2020

# Easy Back-Up of Workflows in WAM

Learn how to easily back up workflows in WAM to protect work, maintain version history, and ensure reliable analytics governance.

For automatic processes and workflows that are updated often, keeping backups is important in case any unwanted changes are made or disaster occurs. WAM can be configured to automatically generate a backup of the workflow whenever an audit report is run. This allows users to keep a copy of the workflow that corresponds to each audit report. These backups are named with a timestamp just like the audit reports, making it easy for users to determine which workflow was used to create each audit report. To specify where these backups and audit reports are saved, go to File > Settings in the WAM interface.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog1.png)

In the Settings window that pops up, select an Output Path by clicking Browse and navigating to where the outputs should be saved, then click Apply. Next, go to the Backups tab in the settings window and ensure that automatic workflow backups are enabled.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog2.png)

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog3.png)

The next time an audit report is run, three folders will be made in the file path specified: Audit Reports, Flow Graphs, and Workflow Backups. A copy of the workflow being audited will be saved in the Workflow Backups folder.

When performing mass updates on workflows, it becomes more important to keep backup copies, since it can be difficult to keep up with every change made. WAM handles the backing up of workflows slightly differently when performing mass updates. Rather than creating a new copy of the workflow, the original workflow is not altered at all. Instead, a new workflow is created that applies the changes made using WAM.

By default, these new workflows have a timestamp included in the name, just like the audit reports. This can be controlled using the Version Control to specify how the new workflow should be named. For example, an accountant uses a workflow monthly to reconcile trial balances but updating the workflow tool by tool to specify the correct month takes quite a bit of time. Using WAM, the accountant can update most of the tools in the workflow at once – changing any month references from August to July. With the version control, the resulting workflow is labelled to show which month it corresponds to, while the original workflow remains unchanged.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog-4.png)

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog5.png)

Now let’s say the workflow contains quarterly calculations that reference month names to determine which quarter the data falls under. In this case, the workflow uses a formula tool to determine which quarter each month falls under like so:

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog6.png)

WAM allows users to specify tool types as well as which items to update (such as field names or expressions). If the mass update is configured to update more than necessary, the formula may be altered incorrectly, as shown below.

![](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/blog-7.png)

Once this happens, the changes cannot be reverted using the mass update tool – thus, keeping backups is crucial when performing automatic changes to a process. Rather than going through every tool or expression in the workflow, the accountant can use the original, unchanged workflow to create a new version – this time making sure to not change the expressions. Keeping backups of workflows for every audit report or mass update ensures that users can keep track of updates and revert any unnecessary or incorrect changes that cause workflows to work incorrectly.
